acceptance_targets: [CRAFT_PETRA_UNEARNED_COVER] Pass if, by the end of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:175-211, a cold reader can point to one concrete piece of technical evidence Petra has seen, one explicit condition or limit she places on Yusuf, and one risk she knowingly accepts by withholding a report. Fail if her cover still rests primarily on sympathy plus backstory.

[DIALOGUE_COMPOSED_INTERROGATION] Pass if the revised dialogue in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:175-211 conveys the same information without any single uninterrupted speech turn functioning as both exposition dump and thematic interpretation, and if at least one Yusuf turn and one Petra turn visibly include interruption, hedging, or self-correction. Fail if the scene still contains polished abstract lines that sound like prepared statements about Meridian, protocols, or physician ethics.

[PROSE_CLIPPED_NOTE_FRAGMENTS] Pass if every sentence in the cited narrative spans reads as complete prose unless it is literal screen text or quoted self-talk, and a reader never has to silently supply a missing 'was,' 'there was,' or equivalent function word to parse the line. Fail if the revised narration still depends on bullet-like fragments for basic action or inference.

[PROSE_STYLE_BIBLE_ECHO] Pass if the revised opening at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:3 still gives Yusuf a concrete coffee detail or fatigue marker but no longer personifies the machine or drink in a way that can be read as a paraphrase of the forbidden example line. Fail if a reader comparing the chapter to the style bible would still flag the coffee joke as an echo.

[CH09_PETRA_STUTTGART_MALE_DECATHLETE] Pass if the Stuttgart patient's name, sport, and gender are identical in Ch 9 and Ch 15. Fail if any detail -- sport, gender, or named vs. anonymous status -- contradicts between the two chapters.
[RED-017] Ch9 'He'-opener rate is below 10%. At least 12 former 'He'-opened sentences now begin with varied constructions.
[length_chapter_09_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, by the end of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:175-211, a cold reader can point to one concrete piece of technical evidence Petra has seen, one explicit condition or limit she places on Yusuf, and one risk she knowingly accepts by withholding a report. Fail if her cover still rests primarily on sympathy plus backstory.
findings.problem: Petra's decision to cover for Yusuf lands as a functional plot bridge before the scene shows what specific proof, limit, or risk makes that choice professionally credible. She moves from catching unauthorized exports and unsanctioned patch work to 'I'm not going to report you' largely on the strength of Yusuf's motive statement and her Stuttgart history, so the chapter asks the reader to take a major alliance shift on trust rather than on an observed decision process.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the turn across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:175-211 so Petra's cover is conditioned by something concrete on the page: a specific test result she inspects, a narrowly defined red line she imposes, a demand to review one export category, or an explicit risk calculation she names before agreeing not to report him. Keep her Stuttgart history if useful, but make it support the decision rather than substitute for it.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised dialogue in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:175-211 conveys the same information without any single uninterrupted speech turn functioning as both exposition dump and thematic interpretation, and if at least one Yusuf turn and one Petra turn visibly include interruption, hedging, or self-correction. Fail if the scene still contains polished abstract lines that sound like prepared statements about Meridian, protocols, or physician ethics.
findings.problem: The Petra-Yusuf confrontation keeps resolving into composed mini-speeches instead of pressured human speech. Yusuf's justification at line 175 becomes a clean thesis about Meridian's motives; Petra's Stuttgart account at lines 183-195 arrives as a tidy diagnostic monologue; and lines 203 and 211 package her stance into polished, quotable formulations. That register checks the chapter's dialogue into thesis-speech and mini-speech diagnosis rather than concealment, interruption, hedging, and social friction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Loosen the exchange in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:175-211. Keep the same factual disclosures, but break the abstract summaries into character-true pressure speech: let Yusuf redirect technically before he admits the personal stake, let Petra's account include more hedging or self-correction, and end her turns on concrete institutional or clinical facts instead of abstract moral phrasing.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if every sentence in the cited narrative spans reads as complete prose unless it is literal screen text or quoted self-talk, and a reader never has to silently supply a missing 'was,' 'there was,' or equivalent function word to parse the line. Fail if the revised narration still depends on bullet-like fragments for basic action or inference.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly slips from shaped close-third narration into clipped pseudo-note fragments: 'Payload diverted. Handshake quarantined.' 'Same refusal. No feed. No elegant compromise.' 'Virus on the left.' 'Same nested error hierarchy.' 'Generic library path. Useless.' 'mbio. Meridian BioSystems.' Repetition makes this read like shorthand bulleting rather than prose, and it violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:39-45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:65-79, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:85-91 into grammatically complete narration or clearly framed on-screen/readout material. Preserve Yusuf's technical rhythm, but make the syntax carry the meaning instead of relying on omitted auxiliaries and note-style fragments.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised opening at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:3 still gives Yusuf a concrete coffee detail or fatigue marker but no longer personifies the machine or drink in a way that can be read as a paraphrase of the forbidden example line. Fail if a reader comparing the chapter to the style bible would still flag the coffee joke as an echo.
findings.problem: The opening coffee-machine joke echoes a non-copyable Yusuf calibration example line from the style bible. 'the corridor machine, which produced an espresso so bitter it felt personal' is too close in construction and effect to the sample voice riff about the machine that tastes like it is mad at you, which makes the opening read as style-bible reuse rather than fresh chapter-specific observation.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the coffee detail in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:3 with a new observation grounded in this night's fatigue, taste, or setting, without reusing the angry-machine/personified-coffee joke structure from the example material.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the Stuttgart patient's name, sport, and gender are identical in Ch 9 and Ch 15. Fail if any detail -- sport, gender, or named vs. anonymous status -- contradicts between the two chapters.

evidence.excerpt: 1769: "Marius Koch. Decathlete. Twenty-four." She was looking at the code on his center display rather than at him. "His body started rejecting the synthetic muscle grafts. Immune cascade, rejection markers all over the bloodwork. The monitoring system kept calling it calibration variance. I let that classification stand for eleven days before I escalated, which — " She stopped. "That's a separate issue."

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 1769: "Marius Koch. Decathlete. Twenty-four." She was looking at the code on his center display rather than at him. "His body started rejecting the synthetic muscle grafts. Immune cascade, rejection markers all over the bloodwork. The monitoring system kept calling it calibration variance. I let that classification stand for eleven days before I escalated, which — " She stopped. "That's a separate issue."

findings.problem: Petra describes her Stuttgart patient as 'Marius Koch. Decathlete. Twenty-four.' and uses male pronouns ('His body started rejecting the synthetic muscle grafts'). In Chapter 15 (line 3145), the same story describes the patient as 'a young gymnast' with female pronouns ('She died eight months later'). This is a triple contradiction -- name/anonymity, sport (decathlete vs. gymnast), and gender (male vs. female) -- in a recurring backstory that anchors Petra's moral authority. Both chapters were likely drafted in parallel without synchronizing this critical detail.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1769, confirm or revise the patient's details to match whatever Chapter 15 establishes. If Ch 15's version (female gymnast) is preferred, change line 1769 to use a female name, 'gymnast,' and female pronouns throughout the passage (lines 1769-1778). If Ch 9's version (Marius Koch, male decathlete) is preferred, the corresponding fix must happen in Ch 15. Choose one version and make it consistent.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch9 'He'-opener rate is below 10%. At least 12 former 'He'-opened sentences now begin with varied constructions.
findings.evidence: Ch9: 32 of 211 sentences (15.2%) begin with 'He'. Manuscript-wide: 285/4041 (7.1%), exceeding the 7% threshold. Ch9 is the single worst offender at more than double the threshold.
findings.problem: 'He' opens 15.2% of sentences in Ch9 — a Yusuf-POV chapter. The pronoun monotony flattens the chapter's rhythm and makes sentence boundaries predictable. The manuscript-wide 'He' rate (7.1%) just exceeds the 7% threshold, and Ch9's 32 'He'-openers are the largest single-chapter contributor.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch9, convert at least 12-15 'He'-opened sentences to alternative constructions: begin with the action, the object, a subordinate clause, or a sensory detail. Target bringing Ch9 below 10% (21 or fewer 'He' openers out of 211 sentences).

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 4000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
